rationale: Major friction on every axis: the blue-lit bedroom UGC palette and casual teen-dance energy directly contradict TheSphinx's mystical-minimalist black/gold premium aesthetic and tense/challenging mood. The hook shows post-play euphoria with zero gameplay surfacing, which fails to communicate the AI questioning/deduction loop or the celebrity-guessing payoff that actually sells this game. Audience skews young/casual ("after school") versus the educated 25-45 intellectual target — only run this if testing a deliberate counter-positioning experiment, otherwise skip in favor of voice-interaction or "can the AI guess who I'm thinking of" demonstration hooks.
